@nicktorba Hypothesis: that drops out when you're optimizing for attention, which organizes around the feed, and that's the attractor for any deviations that aim at a more pleasing/organized/humane experience
@nicktorba my preferred style of twitter is where I can tune into communities, where I can organise people and communities I am interested in without having to overcommit with a follow

@nicktorba But also, I think there's some interface Darwinism that might be in play; I don't think tweetdeck is the mode of browsing **I** want to be in, and maybe its adoption hints that the majority/the big chunk of users want a "different style" that hasnt been realised yet.
@nicktorba maybe. but a part of why we don't have better clients is because Twitter isn't a protocol, and its API is almost allergic to this kind of thing.
